response.justification: This candidate is a web and full-stack engineer whose primary expertise lies in TypeScript, Node.js, and JavaScript. C++ experience is limited to approximately six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) writing components for a C IDE, which falls drastically short of the 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development the role requires. There is no capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking domain knowledge anywhere in the resume, and no mention of SQL Server, Oracle, or Windows-platform development. The candidate's time at RIDE Capital involved Node.js/TypeScript in a fintech-adjacent context, not capital markets or C++ work. The overall profile is misaligned with every mandatory requirement of the role.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) demonstrated across multiple roles
Broad polyglot background including Java, which is listed as a desirable optional skill
Proven communication and knowledge-sharing ability through conference talks and multi-day workshops
concerns: C++ experience amounts to roughly 6 months and is not present in the listed skills section — far below the mandatory 5–10 year requirement
Zero capital markets, fixed income, derivatives, or investment banking domain exposure
No evidence of Windows-platform C++ development, SQL Server, or Oracle — all mandatory technical requirements
